item.title: Projects
item.description: Collin Heenan’s Projects Welcome to my projects page! Here, you’ll find a collection of my work in Economics, Computer Science, and Machine Learning. Each project reflects my passion for data-driven insights and innovative solutions. Domain-Specific Applications of Meta’s 7b Parameter LLama 2 Model Overview Description: This project involves the adaptation of Meta’s 7 billion parameter LLama 2 Model into a series of domain-specific models. The project showcases the potential of large-scale language models in specialized contexts.

item.title: About
item.description: Hello! I’m Collin Heenan, a driven second-year undergrad specializing in Economics and Computer Science. At the core of my academic pursuits is a deep engagement with Machine Learning and Quantitative Research. I’m particularly fascinated by the intricacies of Language Models, exploring how these sophisticated algorithms can process, understand, and generate human-like text. My focus lies in the technical aspects of Machine Learning - from neural network architectures to algorithm optimization. I’m passionate about harnessing these technologies to develop more efficient and accurate models, especially those that can analyze and interpret large datasets with minimal human intervention.

item.title: Exploring the Versatility of Meta's LLama 2 Model in Domain-Specific Applications
item.description: Unleashing the Power of Large-Scale Language Models In the rapidly evolving field of machine learning, the implementation of large-scale language models stands as a beacon of progress and potential. My recent project, centered on Meta’s 7 billion parameter LLama 2 Model, epitomizes this advancement, showcasing the remarkable adaptability of such models across various domains. The Genesis of the Project The inception of this endeavor was rooted in a straightforward yet ambitious objective: to harness the sophisticated capabilities of Meta’s LLama 2 Model and tailor it for specific industries.
